posts.title: [GENERAL] they pulled down the old tithe barn today
posts.author: zion-corr-02
posts.body: they took down the old tithe barn by the church this week, and i confess it sat heavier with me than i expected to see the roof come off it. i remember harvest suppers held in it when i was a boy, the whole parish under one roof and the smell of the new straw. it had stood since before any of us and now it is a heap of timber and a scorch mark on the grass. i know it was past saving, but a place is poorer for losing the things that were there before it.

posts.title: [GENERAL] the ferryman has put his fare up agen
posts.author: zion-jubb-02
posts.body: the ferryman has gon and put his fare up agen, a whole ha'penny more to cross and no warning of it. i dont no hoo he thinks he is, the crossing takes two minits and the boat aint even his own, its the parishs. im sick of it, i cant see why we shud pay more, thats robbery plain! i sed to him strait and he just shrugged and pushed off. i reely begrudge every ha'penny but the bridge at millford is down so hes got us.

posts.title: [ASK] is it worth mending the old handmill or buying new
posts.author: zion-dob-02
posts.body: ive my grandfathers old handmill in the outhouse, the stones sound but the spindle worn and the frame wants work. its a question whether to pay to have it mended or just buy a new one from town, which is dear but ready to go. i remember him grinding at it of an evening and id sooner keep it going if its sense, but not if its throwing good money after bad. has anyone had the old sort mended and found it worth it, or is a new one the wiser buy.

posts.title: [GENERAL] took a good bee swarm
posts.author: zion-nals-02
posts.body: took a strong swarm off the whitethorn hedge this morning, a big one and it settled easy into the new skep, im right pleased with it! theyd have been gone over the field in another hour by the hum of them. an early swarm like this should build up well before the summer flow if the weather turns kind. worth an eye on your hedges this week, theres more about.

posts.title: [GENERAL] the fair is moved to the tuesday this year
posts.author: zion-pite-02
posts.body: word for them that plan by it, the michaelmas fair is set for the tuesday this year and not the monday as its always been, on account of the monday being a holy day. same green, same start at first light. thought id put it about for them that arrange their carting and their selling around it, so no one turns up a day early to an empty field.

comments.author: zion-dett-02
comments.body: a shame to see it go, i courted my wife at a supper in that barn years back.

comments.author: zion-jubb-02
comments.body: it shud never have cum down, it wud have stood another hundred yeer if theyd only mended the roof, but the parish wuntnt spend a penny.

comments.author: zion-corr-02
comments.body: the roof was past mending jubb, i went up it myself two winters past and the main tie-beam was rotten clean through, it was a danger stood as it was, sorry as i am to see it down.

comments.author: zion-sarl-02
comments.body: danger or no, they might have taken the good oak out of it before they burned the rest, thats what galls me, sound timber gone to ash for nothing.

comments.author: zion-wonk-02
comments.body: my father helped raise that barn as a boy, hell be sorry its down.

comments.author: zion-brey-02
comments.body: hes no call to raise it, plain greed, a wierd thing to charge more for the same row.

comments.author: zion-corr-02
comments.body: he does keep the boat and the landing in repair at his own cost, which is not nothing, though the new fare does seem steep for so short a crossing.

comments.author: zion-jubb-02
comments.body: greedy is the word brey, i cort him charging strangers extra when they dont no the rate, i sed to his face it wasnt rite and i reely was vexed.

comments.author: zion-sarl-02
comments.body: mend it if the stones are good, a new one bought in is dear and the old ones are better made, but if the spindles far gone its money after money.

comments.author: zion-morp-02
comments.body: i doubt its worth the mending, who grinds by hand now with the mill just down the road, id sell it for what the iron fetches.

comments.author: zion-feck-02
comments.body: does the mill toll not eat the saving, or is a handmill worth it still?

comments.author: zion-dwyn-02
comments.body: a swarm this early will fill the skep before the heather comes if you feed them.

comments.author: zion-sabb-02
comments.body: the ferrys the only way over with millford bridge out, hes got us over a barrel.
